# 'repo restack -w' only restacks branches
# in stacks that have a branch checked out
# in the current worktree.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# Create two stacks:
# Stack 1: main -> feat1 -> feat2
# Stack 2: main -> feat3 -> feat4

# Stack 1
git add feat1.txt
gs bc feat1 -m 'Add feat1'
git add feat2.txt
gs bc feat2 -m 'Add feat2'

# Stack 2
gs bco main
git add feat3.txt
gs bc feat3 -m 'Add feat3'
git add feat4.txt
gs bc feat4 -m 'Add feat4'

# Create a worktree for feat1 (part of stack 1).
git worktree add ../wt1 feat1

# Update main to require restacking of all branches.
gs bco main
git add main-update.txt
git commit -m 'Update main'

# From wt1, restack only the current worktree's branches.
cd ../wt1
gs repo restack -w
stderr 'feat1: restacked'
! stderr 'feat3'
! stderr 'feat4'

# feat2 is part of the same stack but not checked out here.
# It should still be restacked since it's in the same stack.
stderr 'feat2: restacked'

# Verify that feat3 and feat4 are NOT restacked.
cd ../repo
gs bco feat3
gs branch restack
stderr 'feat3: restacked'

-- repo/feat1.txt --
feat1
-- repo/feat2.txt --
feat2
-- repo/feat3.txt --
feat3
-- repo/feat4.txt --
feat4
-- repo/main-update.txt --
main update
